methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Isomorphic Labs is a private-stage biotech and health company founded by Demis Hassabis with $600M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, and Freenome.

Two primary valuation methods were tested. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable: while 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, none satisfied narrow filters requiring three or more datapoints at matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. Applying the full-sector median would introduce material bias. Public-comp anchoring was also inapplicable because Isomorphic Labs' revenue is not disclosed—a standard constraint for private companies—and the three public comparables yield a sector P/S median of 6.1x, which cannot be meaningfully applied without the target's revenue figure.

Given data limitations, no triangulated valuation range can be responsibly derived. The analysis identifies material information gaps: revenue disclosure from the company or private peers would enable P/S-based valuation, and more granular stage and growth-rate data on the private peer set would unlock peer-median methods. Current capital raised ($600M) provides a floor but reflects investment quantity, not market value. Further diligence should prioritize revenue or ARR data collection before establishing a defensible valuation range.
